Description:
Establish the prompts/ directory as a first-class versioned artefact in the monorepo. Define the XML prompt structure standard, reasoning effort matrix, and CONTRACT.md template. Implement prompt sets for LinkedIn and JobServe agents including prompt-based relevance pre-filtering.

SCOPE:
- prompts/ root scaffold with AGENT.md covering XML structure standard and reasoning effort matrix
- LinkedIn agent prompts: CONTRACT.md, CHANGELOG.md, system.md, skills.md, tools.md, guardrails.md
- JobServe agent prompts: same six-file structure with jobserve-specific selectors and guardrails
- CONTRACT.md template: model pin, reasoning effort, max tokens, temperature, permitted tools, token budget, eval set ref
- Prompt-based relevance pre-filtering integrated into scraper agents before pgvector ranking

MILESTONE: MVP 1.1 — Advanced Prompt Engineering Infrastructure

Create the prompts/ directory and prompts/AGENT.md establishing the prompt engineering standards for the project.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create prompts/ directory at monorepo root
- prompts/AGENT.md content:
  - ## XML Prompt Structure Standard: all prompts must use <system>, <context>, <instructions>, <output_format>, <guardrails> XML tags
  - ## Reasoning Effort Matrix: table mapping task complexity to reasoning effort level (low/medium/high/xhigh) with examples
  - ## Versioning Rules: prompts versioned as MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH in CONTRACT.md CHANGELOG; breaking changes increment MAJOR
  - ## Directory Convention: one subdirectory per agent (e.g. prompts/linkedin-agent/); six mandatory files per agent
  - ## Eval Set Reference: CONTRACT.md must reference the eval set used to validate the prompt version
- Add prompts/ to root AGENT.md index

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompts
DEPENDENCIES: JD-1-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- prompts/ directory exists at monorepo root
- prompts/AGENT.md contains all four sections: XML standard, reasoning effort matrix, versioning rules, directory convention
- Root AGENT.md index updated to include prompts/ entry
- Reasoning effort matrix table renders correctly in markdown

EDGE CASES:
- AGENT.md must be written to be consumed by AI agents as context; avoid ambiguous phrasing
- XML tag names must be consistent across all agent prompt files; document canonical tag set

Create the six-file prompt set for the LinkedIn scraper agent under prompts/linkedin-agent/.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- prompts/linkedin-agent/CONTRACT.md:
  - model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (pinned)
  - reasoning_effort: medium
  - max_tokens: 1000
  - temperature: 0.1
  - permitted_tools: [web_search] (stub; LinkedIn agent uses Playwright not LLM tools in MVP 1)
  - token_budget: 800 input / 200 output
  - eval_set_ref: evals/linkedin-agent/v1.0.0/
  - version: 1.0.0
- prompts/linkedin-agent/CHANGELOG.md: initial 1.0.0 entry with date and summary
- prompts/linkedin-agent/system.md: agent identity, role as LinkedIn job data extractor, output expectations
- prompts/linkedin-agent/skills.md: extraction skills (title parsing, company normalisation, salary extraction)
- prompts/linkedin-agent/tools.md: tool usage instructions (stub for MVP 1; Playwright handles extraction)
- prompts/linkedin-agent/guardrails.md: must not hallucinate fields; must return null for missing data; must not follow redirects to external sites

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompts
DEPENDENCIES: JD-6-1, JD-3-3

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All six files exist under prompts/linkedin-agent/
- CONTRACT.md contains all required fields: model, reasoning_effort, max_tokens, temperature, permitted_tools, token_budget, eval_set_ref, version
- system.md uses correct XML tag structure per prompts/AGENT.md standard
- guardrails.md explicitly prohibits hallucination and missing-field invention

EDGE CASES:
- Model pin must be updated in CONTRACT.md whenever model is upgraded; CHANGELOG.md entry required
- temperature: 0.1 chosen for extraction tasks; document rationale in CONTRACT.md

Create the six-file prompt set for the JobServe scraper agent under prompts/jobserve-agent/.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- prompts/jobserve-agent/CONTRACT.md:
  - model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (pinned)
  - reasoning_effort: medium
  - max_tokens: 1000
  - temperature: 0.1
  - permitted_tools: [] (JobServe agent uses Playwright; no LLM tool calls in MVP 1)
  - token_budget: 800 input / 200 output
  - eval_set_ref: evals/jobserve-agent/v1.0.0/
  - version: 1.0.0
- prompts/jobserve-agent/CHANGELOG.md: initial 1.0.0 entry
- prompts/jobserve-agent/system.md: agent identity as JobServe data extractor; jobserve-specific CSS selectors documented as context
- prompts/jobserve-agent/skills.md: salary string parsing (e.g. '£50,000 - £60,000'), pagination handling, data normalisation to Job schema
- prompts/jobserve-agent/tools.md: stub with note that Playwright handles DOM extraction in MVP 1
- prompts/jobserve-agent/guardrails.md: must not infer salary where not present; must normalise location strings; must not submit duplicate URLs

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompts
DEPENDENCIES: JD-6-1, JD-3-4

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All six files exist under prompts/jobserve-agent/
- CONTRACT.md fields complete and consistent with linkedin-agent CONTRACT.md structure
- system.md references jobserve-specific selectors and pagination behaviour
- guardrails.md includes salary inference prohibition and deduplication rule

EDGE CASES:
- JobServe page structure may change; note in system.md that selectors require periodic review
- Salary parsing edge cases (day rate vs annual) must be documented in skills.md

Create a reusable CONTRACT.md template file that all agent prompt directories must follow.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create prompts/CONTRACT-TEMPLATE.md with all required fields and inline documentation comments:
  - model: <pinned model string> # Never use 'latest'; always pin to exact version
  - reasoning_effort: <low|medium|high|xhigh> # See reasoning effort matrix in prompts/AGENT.md
  - max_tokens: <int> # Set per agent based on expected output size
  - temperature: <float 0.0-1.0> # 0.1 for extraction; 0.7 for generation tasks
  - permitted_tools: [<tool names>] # Empty list if agent uses no LLM tool calls
  - token_budget: <input int> input / <output int> output # Used for cost governance in MVP 2
  - eval_set_ref: evals/<agent-name>/v<version>/ # Path to eval fixtures for this prompt version
  - version: <MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH> # Increment on every prompt change; document in CHANGELOG.md
- Add reference to CONTRACT-TEMPLATE.md in prompts/AGENT.md under ## Directory Convention

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompts
DEPENDENCIES: JD-6-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- prompts/CONTRACT-TEMPLATE.md exists with all 8 required fields
- Each field has an inline comment explaining its purpose and valid values
- prompts/AGENT.md references CONTRACT-TEMPLATE.md
- Existing linkedin-agent and jobserve-agent CONTRACT.md files conform to template

EDGE CASES:
- Template must explicitly state that omitting any field is a CI violation (enforced in Epic 7 eval runner)

Integrate a prompt-based relevance pre-filter into the scraper agents to score and filter job listings before they are stored.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/agents/relevance_filter.py:
  - async def score_relevance(job: Job, keywords: list[str], settings: Settings) -> float
  - Constructs a short prompt using prompts/linkedin-agent/system.md conventions
  - Sends job title + description + keyword list to LLM (claude-sonnet-4-20250514)
  - Parses float score 0.0–1.0 from LLM response
  - Returns score; caller sets job.relevance_score
- Integrate into LinkedInAgent.run() and JobServeAgent.run() after extraction, before add_jobs()
- Threshold: jobs with relevance_score < 0.3 logged and skipped (not stored)
- Threshold configurable via settings.RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD (default 0.3)
- Fallback: if LLM call fails, set relevance_score = 0.5 and store job (fail open)

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend + Prompts
DEPENDENCIES: JD-6-2, JD-6-3, JD-3-3, JD-3-4, JD-2-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Jobs with relevance_score below threshold not added to jobs_store
- Jobs with relevance_score at or above threshold stored with correct score
- LLM failure triggers fail-open: job stored with score 0.5
- RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD setting overrides default correctly
- No Pydantic validation errors on Job.relevance_score field after scoring

EDGE CASES:
- LLM may return score outside 0.0–1.0 range; clamp to valid range before assigning
- High volume scrape runs: batch LLM calls where possible to reduce latency and cost

Implement the evaluation framework for scraper and prompt quality using DeepEval and Ragas. Wire eval runs into the CI pipeline with pass/fail thresholds defined in each agent's CONTRACT.md.

SCOPE:
- backend/admin/run_evals.py: per-agent eval set execution
- DeepEval integration: faithfulness and relevance metrics with CI threshold enforcement
- GitHub Actions CI step: prompt regression check on every merge; blocks deploy if threshold drops

MILESTONE: MVP 1.1 — Advanced Prompt Engineering Infrastructure

Implement backend/admin/run_evals.py as the CLI entry point for running per-agent evaluation sets.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- CLI: uv run python backend/admin/run_evals.py --agent <agent_name> --version <prompt_version>
- Discovers eval fixtures from path referenced in agent's CONTRACT.md eval_set_ref field
- Eval fixture format: JSONL file where each line is {input: {job: Job, keywords: list[str]}, expected_score: float, expected_fields: dict}
- For each fixture: runs relevance_filter.score_relevance() and field extraction; compares output to expected
- Aggregates: pass rate, mean relevance score, field extraction accuracy
- Outputs results to stdout as structured JSON (parseable by CI step)
- Exit code 0 if all thresholds met; exit code 1 if any threshold missed
- Create evals/ directory at monorepo root with evals/linkedin-agent/v1.0.0/ and evals/jobserve-agent/v1.0.0/ containing at least 3 fixture files each

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-6-5, JD-6-4

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Script runs without errors against linkedin-agent v1.0.0 eval set
- Script runs without errors against jobserve-agent v1.0.0 eval set
- Exit code 1 when pass rate below threshold
- Exit code 0 when all fixtures pass
- Output JSON parseable by jq

EDGE CASES:
- Missing eval_set_ref path: script exits with clear error message and non-zero code
- Partial fixture failures: report all failures before exiting, not just first failure

Integrate DeepEval to measure faithfulness and relevance metrics for agent prompt outputs.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add deepeval to backend/pyproject.toml dependencies
- Create backend/evals/deepeval_runner.py:
  - faithfulness_metric: LLMTestCase comparing generated job description summary against source text; threshold from CONTRACT.md
  - relevance_metric: measures cosine similarity between job fields and keyword set; threshold from CONTRACT.md
  - run_deepeval(agent_name: str, fixtures: list[EvalFixture]) -> DeepEvalResult
  - DeepEvalResult: { agent: str; faithfulness_score: float; relevance_score: float; passed: bool }
- Integrate deepeval_runner into run_evals.py: called after basic fixture pass/fail check
- Threshold values read from CONTRACT.md at runtime (parse YAML-like fields from markdown)
- Log per-metric results via get_logger(__name__)

EFFORT: L (7 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-7-1, JD-6-4

TESTING CRITERIA:
- deepeval installed and importable in backend virtual environment
- faithfulness_metric produces score between 0.0 and 1.0
- relevance_metric produces score between 0.0 and 1.0
- DeepEvalResult.passed reflects both metrics meeting their thresholds
- CONTRACT.md threshold parsing works for both linkedin-agent and jobserve-agent

EDGE CASES:
- DeepEval requires LLM API key at runtime; document in .env.example as OPENAI_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
- Metric computation may be slow; add --fast flag to run_evals.py that skips DeepEval and runs fixture-only checks

Add a prompt regression CI step to GitHub Actions that runs evals on every merge to main and blocks deployment if thresholds drop.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add job prompt-regression to .github/workflows/ci.yml:
  - Trigger: on push to main only (not on PR to avoid excessive LLM API usage)
  - Steps:
    - Checkout repository
    - Set up Python with uv
    - Install backend dependencies
    - Run: uv run python backend/admin/run_evals.py --agent linkedin-agent --version 1.0.0
    - Run: uv run python backend/admin/run_evals.py --agent jobserve-agent --version 1.0.0
  - Requires secrets: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (set in GitHub repo secrets)
  - On failure: annotate PR with which agent and metric failed
- prompt-regression job added as required status check before docker-build on main merges
- Add ## CI Eval Thresholds section to docs/EXECUTION-RULES.md documenting current passing thresholds

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: JD-7-2, JD-5-4

TESTING CRITERIA:
- prompt-regression job triggers on push to main
- Job fails and blocks merge if faithfulness or relevance score drops below CONTRACT.md threshold
- Job passes when all thresholds met
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret used correctly and not exposed in logs
- Failure annotation appears on GitHub Actions summary page

EDGE CASES:
- LLM API rate limits during CI: add retry logic with exponential backoff in run_evals.py
- CI cost control: prompt-regression runs only on main push, not on every PR branch push
